retrieval_text: Dandelion is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 279 works and 644 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Dandelion, Dandelions. Common plant-part co-mentions include Root, Bark, Leaf, Seed, Flower, Gum. Common preparation co-mentions include Extract, Pill, Decoction, Juice, Powder, Oil. Common condition co-mentions include Cold or catarrh, Dropsy, Inflammation, Fever, Consumption, Constipation. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Child-use caution, Bleeding caution, Toxicity watch, Allergy or sensitivity caution. Representative source works include The pharmacopoeia universalis or, complete encyclopedia of the materia medica; contained in the pharmacopoeias of London, Edinburgh, and Dublin as well as of all those of Europe and America ; and of the dispensatories, formularies and chemical works ... With an appendix ... / by A. J. L. Jourdan : Edited by J.
